text: Meeting deadlines is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. The first step is effective time management. Break your project into smaller tasks and set mini-deadlines for each one. Next, learn prioritization—identify the most urgent or important tasks and complete them first. If a deadline seems impossible, practice scope management: negotiate what can be delivered realistically without sacrificing quality. Finally, communication is key. If you foresee a delay, inform your team or supervisor early. It’s better to be honest and request an extension than to submit rushed work. By combining these strategies, you can handle deadlines with confidence and reduce stress.
